title: Migrating from v2.x Contacts to v3.x Contacts
heading: Migrating from v2.x Contacts to v3.x Contacts
parentHeadings: Migrating from v2.x Contacts to v3.x Contacts
content:
/\*! tailwindcss v4.1.17 | MIT License | https://tailwindcss.com \*/ @layer theme, base, components, utilities; @layer utilities;

## 

Overview

[](#overview)

With the release of Clever API v3.0, contact data has undergone significant updates. Notably, all contact users now receive a new identifier, the Clever Multi-Role User ID. The previous Clever ID can still be accessed via the `legacy_id` field, similar to other user types in Clever.

In addition, to address the case of contacts that are associated with multiple students in different capacities, a `student_relationships` array was introduced. The API v2 contacts endpoint assigns a single relationship to each contact, which can result in inaccurate or misleading data when a contact is associated with multiple students.  
API v3 resolves this issue by allowing contacts to have distinct relationships for each associated student, ensuring accurate and reliable data.

Follow the guidelines below to update your application to use v3 endpoints for contact data.

* * *

## 

Key Changes in v3 Contact Endpoints

[](#key-changes-in-v3-contact-endpoints)

The table below highlights the v2.x to v3.x endpoint replacements:

v2.x

v3.x

`GET /contacts`

`GET /users?role=contact`

`GET /contacts/{id}`

`GET /users/{id}`

`GET /contacts/{id}/students`

`GET /users/{id}/mystudents`

`GET /contacts/{id}/district`

`GET /users/{id}/district`

`GET /students/{id}/contacts`

`GET /users/{id}/mycontacts`

`GET /events?record_type=contacts`

`GET /events?record_type=users`

### 

Events API

[](#events-api)

If you're calling the /v2.x/events endpoint to obtain contact data, you'll need to update this flow to retrieve contacts from the v3 endpoints. You can do this in a few ways:

-   **Pull the associated contacts for a single student**
    -   e.g., `/v3.0/users/{id}/mycontacts`
-   **Pull contact data in bulk**
    -   e.g., `/v3.0/users?role=contact`
-   **Pull contact data from v3.0 Events API**
    -   e.g., `/v3.0/events?record_type=users`
    -   Filter for events for users with a Contact role, still adhering to the Event Ordering specified [here](https://dev.clever.com/docs/events-api#processing-events).

* * *

## 

New Data Structure for Contact Relationships

[](#new-data-structure-for-contact-relationships)

-   **Student Relationships Array**: Contacts now include a `student_relationships` array, detailing all relationships the contact has with students in their district's Student Information System (SIS).
    -   Each entry in the array represents a specific relationship, identified by both the **relationship type** and the **Clever ID of the student**.
    -   For example, if a contact is listed as a parent for two students and an aunt to another, they will have one Clever ID and three entries in the `student_relationships` array.

> 🚧
> 
> ### 
> 
> Important Consideration
> 
> [](#important-consideration)
> 
> Ensure each relationship entry in `student_relationships` is accurately tied to the correct student ID when managing privileges in your database

### 

Example Contact Record

[](#example-contact-record)

A sample contact data response in v3.0 includes:

```
{
  "data": {
    "created": "2022-11-28T18:46:36.819Z",
    "district": "6385008f69df036e83e984a3",
    "email": "[email protected]",
    "last_modified": "2024-11-04T21:05:06.631Z",
    "name": {
      "last": "Aaron Collins"
    },
    "id": "63850203bfb8460546071ef6", //Clever API v3.x ID
    "roles": {
      "contact": {
        "phone": "1-(330) 280-1898",
        "phone_type": "Other",
        "sis_id": "",
        "student_relationships": [
          {
            "relationship": "Aunt/Uncle",
            "student": "63850203bfb8460546071e62",
            "type": "Family"
          },
          {
            "relationship": "Aunt/Uncle",
            "student": "672936fc65fc4500ced13260",
            "type": "Family"
          },
          {
            "relationship": "Parent",
            "student": "63850203bfb8460546071e6e",
            "type": "Parent/Guardian"
          }
        ],
        "legacy_id": "63850203bfb8460546071e7e" //Clever API v2.x ID
      }
    }
  },
  "uri": "/v3.0/users/63850203bfb8460546071ef6"
},
```

This example demonstrates the structure of a contact's data in API v3.0. The `student_relationships` array lists each relationship the contact has with specific students, identified by both the `relationship` type and the student's Clever ID (`student`).

By following these guidelines, you’ll ensure a smooth transition to API v3.0 for contact data in your application. If you encounter any issues, consult the linked documentation or reach out to our support team.

See below for additional resources on upgrading to API v3.0 and the v3.0 user object changes.
